subject: Re: max number of frames with K3 data?
body:
Ok, it's fixed now, and an old Titan X with 12 GB seems to be enough for 
the full pipeline, assuming 2x downsampling of the superres data. I'll 
share the updated installer once I've accumulated a few more fixes.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Tuesday, April 23, 2019 at 10:21:27 AM UTC+2, Dimitry Tegunov wrote:
>
> Hi Matt,
>
> sorry for the delay, I'm still fixing the bugs this test uncovered (things 
> being 32bit that should have been 64bit). CTF estimation works fine now, 
> but I have yet to get to motion estimation.
>
> That aside, 120 frames in superres will create an IO bottleneck that is 
> hard to solve. Thoroughly comparing the benefits of superres is likely 
> worth the time it will save you if the benefit turns out negligible (Warp 
> uses higher-order interpolation, so the interpolation argument made for MC2 
> doesn't hold). 
>
> If the intent of having 120 frames is finer dose fractionation, I seem to 
> remember Gatan talking about variable frame rate for the K3 a few years 
> ago. If it's available in the final product, implementing support in Warp 
> would be easy – then you'd only need to fractionate the early electrons 
> more finely. If, on the other hand, the intent is to have a higher overall 
> dose for a difficult sample, perhaps testing Warp's denoising and picking 
> on lower-dose images is worthwhile.
>
> Cheers,
> Dimitry
>
> On Tuesday, April 23, 2019 at 6:18:12 AM UTC+2, Matt Harrington wrote:
>>
>> I tried again with an AWS p3.2xlarge instance, which has a V100 with 
>> 16 GB VRAM.  Got the following exception: 
>>
>> Running on GPU #0 (15282 MB free) through WarpWorker0 
>>
>> Received "SetHeaderlessParams", with 3 arguments, for GPU #0, 15282 MB 
>> free 
>> Set headerless parameters to 11520, 8184, 0, int16 
>>
>> Received "LoadGainRef", with 4 arguments, for GPU #0, 15282 MB free 
>> Loaded gain reference: 11520, 8184, 1, normal, real, 1 A/px, ID = 0, 
>> False, False, False 
>>
>> Received "LoadStack", with 2 arguments, for GPU #0, 15282 MB free 
>> Loaded stack: 5760, 4092, 120, normal, real, 1 A/px, ID = 1, 0.5 
>>
>> Received "MovieProcessCTF", with 2 arguments, for GPU #0, 15028 MB free 
>>
>> Unhandled Exception: System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to 
>> read or write protected memory. This is often an indication that other 
>> memory is corrupt. 
>>    at Warp.GPU.CopyHostToHost(Single[] h_source, IntPtr h_dest, Int64 
>> elements) 
>>    at Warp.Image.GetHostPinned(Intent intent) 
>>    at Warp.Movie.ProcessCTF(Image originalStack, 
>> ProcessingOptionsMovieCTF options) 
>>    at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) 
>>
>
